query: EU AI Act AI Omnibus machinery scope exemption sectoral regulations overlap agreement details

{"query":"EU AI Act AI Omnibus machinery scope exemption sectoral regulations overlap agreement details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1590,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://iapp.org/news/a/eu-agrees-to-amend-ai-act-clarifies-overlap-with-machinery-rules","title":"EU agrees to amend AI Act, clarifies overlap with machinery rules | IAPP","content":"As a result, machinery is exempted from direct AI Act applicability where overlap exists, with AI-specific health and safety handled through the Machinery Regulation.
The deal extends some regulatory exemptions that were provided to SMEs to small mid-cap companies.
In comments to the IAPP, Kai Zenner, head of office and digital policy adviser for German MEP Axel Voss, said, "The AI Omnibus negotiations are over, giving companies more time to get compliant and removing machinery products largely from the law's scope." Though the updates are a "positive" step, Zenner said these "minor steps will not solve the structural problems — the EU's digital acquis (the EU's Digital Rulebook) still requires a major overhaul, in particular, when it comes to legal coherence as well as the enforcement of our rules.""},{"url":"https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai","title":"AI Act | Shaping Europe’s digital future","content":"The AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence) is the first-ever comprehensive legal framework on AI worldwide.
The Guidelines on transparent AI systems to clarify the scope of application, relevant legal definitions, the transparency obligations, the exceptions and related horizontal issues.
This legislative proposal (dubbed as the 'AI omnibus') has been adopted on 19 November 2025 and a political agreement was reached on 7 May 2026."},{"url":"https://www.gibsondunn.com/eu-ai-act-omnibus-agreement-postponed-high-risk-deadlines-and-other-key-changes/","title":"EU AI Act Omnibus Agreement — Postponed High-Risk Deadlines and Other Key Changes - Gibson Dunn","content":"High-risk obligations for stand-alone Annex III systems are deferred to 2 December 2027; for AI embedded in regulated products under Annex I, to 2 August 2028.
By late 2025, implementation was visibly off track, prompting the European Commission to table the Digital Omnibus on AI on 19 November 2025 (see our previous client alert on the Commission’s Digital Omnibus proposal).
Furthermore, AI systems embedded in products covered by the Machinery Regulation will be largely exempted from the AI Act obligations.
The Article 50 transparency obligations are largely unaffected by the Omnibus."},{"url":"https://thelens.slaughterandmay.com/post/102ms2v/eu-agree-simplification-of-ai-rules","title":"EU Agree simplification of AI rules, Natalie Donovan","content":"The omnibus changes narrow down what qualify as “safety components”, meaning that products with AI functions that only assist users, or optimise performance, will not automatically be subject to high-risk obligations.
The compromise limits the application of the Act where sectoral law has similar AI-specific requirements, and exempts the Machinery Regulation from direct applicability of the Act.
While these changes were specified in the press releases from the Commission, Council and Parliament on the political agreement, we also expect to see changes in other areas, such as the AI literacy rules (see our blog here for more details)."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/part-1-navigating-eu-ai-act-2026-digital-omnibus-new-regulatory-iayee","title":"PART 1: Navigating the EU AI Act in 2026: The Digital Omnibus and the New Regulatory Reality","content":"On 5 February 2026, the European Commission unveiled the Digital Omnibus simplification package.
Fast forward to 7 May 2026, and a major milestone was reached: the European Parliament and the Council of the EU reached a political agreement on this package
These organizations are exempt from the heaviest AI governance and quality management system obligations (Article 17)."},{"url":"https://www.globalpolicywatch.com/2026/05/eu-ai-act-update-timeline-relief-targeted-simplification-and-new-prohibitions/","title":"EU AI Act Update: Timeline Relief, Targeted Simplification, ...","content":"May 19, 2026 — Annex III HRAIS (use-based): Obligations are postponed from 2 August 2026 to 2 December 2027 (i.e., deferred by 16 months). · Machinery ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"EU AI Act AI Omnibus machinery scope exemption sectoral regulations overlap agreement details","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://foxxelabs.ie/news/eu-ai-omnibus-deal-reshapes-machinery-regulation-interplay-why-irish-manufacturers-must-rethink-product-safety-compliance","title":"EU AI Omnibus Deal Reshapes Machinery Regulation Interplay: Why Irish Manufacturers Must Rethink Product Safety Compliance | Foxxe Labs","content":"# EU AI Omnibus Deal Reshapes Machinery Regulation Interplay: Why Irish Manufacturers Must Rethink Product Safety Compliance. May 2026 agreement clarifies AI Act overlap with EU product safety laws, creating new compliance pathways for Irish industrial AI deployments. #EU AI Act  #Machinery Regulation  #Product Safety  #Irish Compliance. ## EU AI Omnibus Deal Clarifies Machinery Regulation Overlap—What It Means for Irish Manufacturers. The May 7, 2026 provisional agreement on the Digital Omnibus on AI includes a critical but underreported clarification: the interplay between the EU AI Act and product safety laws, particularly the Machinery Regulation, is now explicitly defined to avoid duplication. This distinction matters enormously for Irish and European manufacturers deploying AI in machinery, industrial automation, and safety-critical systems—yet few builders fully understand what it means for their compliance roadmap. The amendment agreement between the Council, Parliament, and Commission establishes clearer boundaries between sectoral AI rules (like the Machinery Regulation) and the AI Act’s horizontal framework.","score":0.83452857,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://iapp.org/news/a/eu-agrees-to-amend-ai-act-clarifies-overlap-with-machinery-rules","title":"EU agrees to amend AI Act, clarifies overlap with machinery rules | IAPP","content":"The European Parliament and Council of the European Union reached a provisional agreement to reform the AI Act, part of the Omnibus on AI simplification package. One week after negotiations between European Parliament and the Council of the European Union were delayed, co-legislators reached a provisional agreement to reform the Artificial Intelligence Act, part of the Digital Omnibus on AI simplification package. A looming compliance deadline on high-risk AI systems placed significant pressure on reaching an agreement in time. The European Commission would be able to adopt delegated acts to add health and safety requirements for AI systems that are deemed high risk under the AI Act. Regarding potential overlap between the act and other sectoral laws — including for medical devices, connected cars and toys — the European Commission would be charged with creating implementing acts.","score":0.79883134,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://thelens.slaughterandmay.com/post/102ms2v/eu-agree-simplification-of-ai-rules","title":"EU Agree simplification of AI rules, Natalie Donovan - The Lens","content":"The compromise limits the application of the Act where sectoral law has similar AI-specific requirements, and exempts the Machinery Regulation","score":0.6607204,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/alisarmustafa1_eu-agrees-to-amend-ai-act-clarifies-overlap-activity-7467983758114148352-oQ1W","title":"Alisar Mustafa's Post","content":"EU agrees to amend AI Act, clarifies overlap with machinery rules ▷ The European Parliament and the Council of the European Union reached a","score":0.5942929,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://cms.law/en/bel/legal-updates/eu-ai-act-developments-key-political-agreement-on-the-digital-omnibus-on-ai-implementation-timeline-and-transparency-consultation","title":"EU AI Act Developments: Key Political Agreement on the ...","content":"Removing the EU Machinery Regulation from the AI Act's direct scope. This means AI in machinery will not have to follow all AI Act high-risk","score":0.568057,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.86,"request_id":"1c33d6ed-f8ca-4008-852f-88dba0e501b2"}
